name: Vegiburgers in Pita Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 3 cups of water to a boil in a heavy large pot.
2. Stir in barley, lentils, and rice. Reduce heat to low. Cover and cook until grains are tender, about 4 minutes.
3. Transfer to a large bowl. Drain and cool.
4. Heat 1/4 cup oil in a heavy large skillet over medium-low heat. Add carrots, onion, celery, sunflower seeds, and garlic. Sauté until vegetables are tender, about 12 minutes.
5. Mix in basil, thyme, and oregano. Season with salt and pepper. (Can be made up to 1 day ahead. Cover and refrigerate.)
6. Preheat oven to 300°F.
7. Cut tops off pita rounds to form pockets. Wrap in foil and place in oven to warm.
8. Stir beaten eggs and flour into grain mixture. Press about half the mixture between palms of hands to form patties. Repeat with remaining mixture.
9. Heat a film of oil in a heavy nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add patties in batches and cook until golden brown and heated through, about 5 minutes per side.
10. Place 1 patty in each pita round. Top with lettuce and tomatoes and serve.
